name: SunCulture Drip Irrigation Kit
description: The SunCulture AgroSolar Irrigation Kit is a combination of solar pumping technology and a drip irrigation system.

taxonomy.sub_category: SunCulture Drip Irrigation Kit Updated on January 20, 2024 · Created on August 18, 2016 SunCulture Drip Irrigation Kit The SunCulture AgroSolar Irrigation Kit is a combination of solar pumping technology and a drip irrigation system. Developed By SunCulture Tested By SunCulture Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The SunCulture Drip Irrigation Kit is a low pressure, gravity fed, drip-irrigation system suitable for plots ranging from 1/8 acre to 1 acre in size. Drip irrigation has been proven to help save water, increase crop yields, and decrease weed growth. SunCulture makes fertigation possible by including a fertilizer injection system with each kit. This allows farmers to reduce fertilizer and nutrient losses due to localized application and minimized leaching. Target SDGs SDG 1: No Poverty SDG 2: Zero Hunger Market Suggested Retail Price $856.49 Market Suggested Retail Price (Secondary Currency) 89000.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Community, Small and Medium-sized Enterprises Distributors / Implementing Organizations SunCulture Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include SunCulture SP-300 Solar Pumping System , Netakit , and Proximity Designs Drip Irrigation System . Regions East Africa Countries Tanzania, Zambia Manufacturing/Building Method The product is mass manufactured in the US. Intellectural Property Type Trade Secret User Provision Model Users can obtain this product directly from SunCulture. SunCulture offers a free quote to farmers who submit their information online, and also have a technician available to visit customer sites that require consultation for a customized system. SunCulture partners with Equity Bank to provide financing options to farmers who cannot afford to pay for the system upfront. Distributions to Date Status SunCulture has over 650 customers in East Africa.Interview with representative Coefficient of distribution uniformity +/- 10% interview with representative Highest limit burst pressure Should not exceed pressure over 1 bar. Maximum run length 50 m interview with representative Minimum viable operating pressure 0.3 bar Design Specifications The Product combines a solar pumping technology (solar panels providing pump's electricity directly without need for batteries) with an efficient drip irrigation system. This system includes long lasting drip-tape, a filtration system, fertilizer injection system, HDPE and PVC pipes, and all needed connectors, fittings, and valves. The pump pulls water from different sources (lake, river, stream, well, boreal, water harvester, etc) to a raised storage tank during the day. In the evening a valve is opened and water flows with gravity to irrigate the farm. Technical Support SunCulture provides training and after-sales support upon request. Replacement Components An entire after sales process is dedicated to supporting customers with needed replacement parts, as well as system repairs if necessary. Interview with representative Lifecycle The solar modules comes with a 20 year warranty Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters SunCulture estimates farmers can make up to 4x the revenue with irrigated agriculture compared to traditional agriculture, and 2x the revenue of rain-fed agriculture. See below. Vetted Performance Status Unknown Safety No known safety hazards are related to this product. Complementary Technical Systems This product was available as an add-on for SunCulture's AgroSolar irrigation system. Another pumping system such as the SP-300 Solar Pumping System can also be used. Academic Research and References None Compliance with regulations Unknown Other Information SunCulture has won multiple awards for their systems and work, including: FT/IF Transformational Business Award 2018 , Finalist in 2017 of Ashden International Award Finalist in 2017 of Start Up Energy Transition Award: Category; “Start Up SDG7” Finalist in 2016 of Ocean Exchange, The Solutions Awards, Winner in 2015 of Sustainia Award, Food Innovation Category, Winner in 2015 of "Powering Agriculture: An Energy Grand Challenge for Development" SunCulture - Scaling the Distribution of Tailored Agro-Solar Irrigation Kits to Smallholder Farmers Price for kits differ depending on size of farm. It ranges from 249 USD (KES 24, 900) to 890 USD (KES 89,000) for 1/8 to 1 full acre. Prices converted to USD 06/03/2019 SunCulture's AgroSolar irrigation system Design Specifications and product solar modules Product mass production in US For schematic diagram, see the AgroSolar Irrigation System Leave a Reply You must be logged in to post a comment.
